description: Mailbox state (kind 30099) test vectors for NOSTR Mail. State is stored as a JSON payload encrypted with NIP-44 to the user's own pubkey in the content field. Only the d-tag (YYYY-MM partition) is visible to relays. State uses a G-Set (grow-only set) for reads/deleted and LWW for flags/folders.

crdt_model.reads: G-Set (grow-only set). Once a message is marked read, it cannot be marked unread. Union of all read sets across devices.
crdt_model.flags: Union per message ID. All flags from all devices are preserved on merge.
crdt_model.folders: LWW (last-writer-wins). The newer state's folder assignment wins for each message ID.
crdt_model.deleted: G-Set. Once marked deleted, cannot be undeleted via state merge.

payload_schema.description: The decrypted content field of a kind 30099 event is a JSON object with this structure:

fields.read: string[] — Array of message-id values for read messages (G-Set)
fields.flag: Record<string, string[]> — Map of message-id to flag name arrays
fields.folder: Record<string, string> — Map of message-id to folder name
fields.deleted: string[] — Array of message-id values for deleted messages (G-Set)

vectors.name: mark message as read
vectors.description: Bob reads a message on his phone. The state payload is updated to include the new message-id in the read array.

expected_payload.note: The new message-id is appended to the read array. Existing entries are preserved (G-Set grows monotonically).

expected_event.note: Only the d-tag is visible to relays. All state is inside the encrypted content.

vectors.name: flag a message
vectors.description: Bob stars a message. The flag map is updated.

expected_payload.note: The flag map gains an entry mapping the message-id to an array of flag names.

vectors.name: move message to folder
vectors.description: Bob moves a message from inbox to the 'Work' folder.

expected_payload.note: Messages without a folder entry are implicitly in the inbox. A message can only be in one folder; moving to a new folder replaces the existing entry.

vectors.name: merge two state payloads (multi-device conflict resolution)
vectors.description: Bob reads message C on his phone (Device 1) and stars message A on his laptop (Device 2) at roughly the same time. Both devices publish state updates. The client must merge them.

payload_device_2.note: Device 2 added: starred(A), folder(A -> Personal). Device 2 has later created_at.

merge_rules_applied.reads: G-Set union: {A, B} union {A, B, C} = {A, B, C}. Read C from Device 1 is preserved.
merge_rules_applied.flags: Union: starred(A) from Device 2 is preserved.
merge_rules_applied.folders: LWW: Device 2 is newer, so folder(A -> Personal) wins over folder(A -> Work).
merge_rules_applied.note: Reads and deleted use G-Set union (always grow). Flags use set union per message. Folders use LWW (newer device wins).

vectors.name: attempt to unread a message (no-op for G-Set)
vectors.description: Bob accidentally reads a message and wants to mark it as unread. Because reads use a G-Set, the read entry cannot be removed through merge.

expected_merge_behavior.note: G-Set merge will always re-add the read entry because the other device's state still contains it.

workaround.description: To support 'mark as unread' as a user-facing feature, use a 'needs-attention' flag that overrides the read state at the UI level.
workaround.ui_interpretation: If a message has 'needs-attention' flag, display as unread even though it is in the G-Set read array.
